stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 83.4 °C and a standard deviation of 5.5 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 3.2% and 7.5%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 83.4 °C. After correcting for the 5.3% bias: ≈ 88.1 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.2%-7.5%) and sampling variability: [82.7, 93.7]. Point estimate: 88.1 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
